LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF WRITTEN GUARANTY). THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST (BREACH OF CONTRACT), THIRD (SERVICES SOLD AND PERFORMED); FOURTH (OPEN ACCOUNT); FIFTH (ACCOUNT STATED); SIXTH (QUANTUM MERUIT); AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION (UNJUST ENRICHMENT). IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THE ERRORS IN ITS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION AND PROPOSES TO FILE A THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT: (1) HAS LEGIBLE COPIES OF THE WRITTEN CONTRACTS; (2) REMOVES MR. PRESCOTT AS A PARTY TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION; AND (3) ELIMINATES THE FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THEIR ENTIRETY. LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED FOR PLAINTIFF TO FILE THE PROPOSED THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ATTACHED TO ITS OPPOSITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: douglas provencher, esq., 707.284.2380, for plaintiff creditors trade association, inc.
jonathan van ee, esq., 415.938.7694, for defendant roy prescott.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege the second cause of action for breach of written guaranty. The pleadings are not uncertain. Defendant raises questions of fact that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: douglas provencher, esq., 707.284.2380, for plaintiff creditors trade association, inc.
jonathan van ee, esq., 415.938.7694, for defendant roy prescott.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. As pled, the prayer for relief for interest and attorney fees are not irrelevant, false or improper. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JONATHAN VAN EE, ESQ., 415.938.7694, FOR DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Leave to amend has been granted for Plaintiff to file a proposed Third Amended Complaint which will have legible copies of attachments. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JONATHAN VAN EE, ESQ., 415.938.7694, FOR DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second cause of action (breach of written guaranty). The demurrer is sustained, without leave to amend as to the first (breach of contract), third (services sold and performed); fourth (open account); fifth (account stated); sixth (quantum meruit); and seventh causes of action (unjust enrichment). In opposition, Plaintiff concedes the errors in its Second Amended Complaint as to these causes of action and proposes to file a Third Amended Complaint that: (1) has legible copies of the written contracts; (2) removes Mr. Prescott as a party to the first cause of action; and (3) eliminates the fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action in their entirety. Leave to amend is granted for Plaintiff to file the proposed Third Amended Complaint attached to its opposition. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
